characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Affectionate Nickname: Ranger Rabbit sometimes refers to Elinor as "Honey Bunny." Animal Stereotypes: Averted. She is an only child, meaning her parents have not"multiplied like rabbits". Break the Cutie: In "Junior Ranger Elinor," Ranger Rabbit designates Elinor as a junior ranger, and Elinor promises to protect the forest. When a fire ruins the forest, Elinor feels like she's failed at her mission, and she runs away to cry. This is surprising for such a happy-go-lucky character. Luckily, her mom cheers her up by saying that the forest can grow back after a fire. Bunnies for Cuteness: Her being a bunny, herConstantly Curiousnature, and her voice all make her adorable. Catchphrase:After Elinor, Olive and Ari have explored something a bit, Elinor likes to declare "We need more observations!"After learning something cool, Elinor comments "That issointeresting!"

characters.name: Elinor Rabbit
characters.description: Affectionate Nickname: Ranger Rabbit sometimes refers to Elinor as "Honey Bunny." Animal Stereotypes: Averted. She is an only child, meaning her parents have not"multiplied like rabbits". Break the Cutie: In "Junior Ranger Elinor," Ranger Rabbit designates Elinor as a junior ranger, and Elinor promises to protect the forest. When a fire ruins the forest, Elinor feels like she's failed at her mission, and she runs away to cry. This is surprising for such a happy-go-lucky character. Luckily, her mom cheers her up by saying that the forest can grow back after a fire. Bunnies for Cuteness: Her being a bunny, herConstantly Curiousnature, and her voice all make her adorable. Catchphrase:After Elinor, Olive and Ari have explored something a bit, Elinor likes to declare "We need more observations!"After learning something cool, Elinor comments "That issointeresting!"

characters.name: Olive Elephant
characters.description: Ambiguously Absent Parent: While Olive's mother makes frequent appearances, Olive's father is nowhere to be seen. It can be assumed that they are divorced and Olive's mom is a single parent, given how she's referred to as Ms. Elephant. Appropriate Animal Attire: "The Science of Staying Warm" discusses the fact that Olive, due to not having fur, has to wear lots of layers during winter. Blue Means Smart One: She's the smart one in Elinor's friend group, often sharing facts she finds in her nature book. Bookworm: She loves reading books. She even dressed up as a book in one episode, and aLiteral Bookwormin another.Olive: I like reading, especially my nature book. Color Motif: Her outfits tend to be blue, which represents her femininity and intellect. "Feathers" reveals that her favorite color is blue.

characters.name: Ari Bat
characters.description: Acrophobic Bird: Ari, being a bat, likes flying high, but in "Flying High," when the hot air balloon gets higher than he's ever flown before, he gets nervous. Appropriate Animal Attire: Zig-zagged. Despite being a bat, Ari apparently has no problems wearing clothes with normal sleeves (it is possible, however, that they are modified to fit his wings). Big Eater: Ari is regularly snacking on something. In "Ms. Mole's Glasses," when Ms. Mole calls him out for reaching for food without waiting for the others to sit down first, Elinor and Olive wonder just what sense she used to know that he was doing it, given that she was looking in the other direction at the time. She points out that it's a matter of simple deduction that Ari is always hungry. Borrowed Catchphrase: In the short "That's So Interesting - How Do Cheetahs Run So Fast" Ari declares "We need more observations!" saying that it's what Elinor would say. Boyish Shorts: He'sThe One Guyof the main trio and he wears yellow shorts.

characters.name: Ranger Rabbit
characters.description: Alliterative Name:RangerRabbit. Color Motifs: She is associated with light blue and green. When she's not wearing her ranger uniform, her outfits, like her casual outfit, her pajamas, and her swimsuit, incorporate these colors. Good Parents: She is happy to answer Elinor's questions and makes sure she stays safe. Tomboyish Ponytail: Flashbacks in "A Wonderful Journey" show that she wore her hair in a ponytail when she was younger. Uniformity Exception: Unlike her father and daughter, her ears are pure white instead of being filled in with pink.

characters.name: Mr. Rabbit
characters.description: Exhausted Eye Bags: He has these in "Bird Song" after being woken up early by a bird loudly singing outside the window. Good Parents: He's a caring father to Elinor. Obliquely Obfuscated Occupation: A few episodes feature him wearing a casual business outfit with a tie. His job, however, is unknown and is never seen or mentioned in the series. Talking in Your Sleep:He tweets in his sleep in "Bird Song" after falling asleep at the table during breakfast because he was woken up early by a bird singing outside the window.In "Light the Way," he falls asleep after eating marshmallows. After being disturbed by Olive trumpeting after accidentally stepping on a stick, he murmurs "Uh, more marshmallows? Why yes, please." Later in the episode, after being disturbed again, he says "That's the biggest marshmallow I've ever seen." He tweets in his sleep in "Bird Song" after falling asleep at the table during breakfast because he was woken up early by a bird singing outside the window.

characters.name: Ms. Elephant
characters.description: Caring Gardener: She has flower gardens at her house, and if she's in an episode, she's likely dealing with flowers. Good Parents: Like Elinor's parents, Olive's mom is gentle and kind. Hidden Depths: She is talented at playing the violin. Same Clothes, Different Year: An old photo shown in "Spice is Nice" reveals that Ms. Elephant has been wearing the same earrings since she was little. Unnamed Parent: Like the other parents in the show, her first name is unknown.

characters.name: Koa Wombat
characters.description: A Day in the Limelight: He is the main focus of "Foody Fuel." Eyes Always Shut: Koa's eyes are usually shut due to his excessive fur. Sometimes he opens them. For some reason, in "The Seed of An Idea", his eyes are open the whole time like everyone else. Mellow Fellow: He is very laid-back, refers to things as "chill," and talks like a little surfer dude. Trademark Favorite Food: In "Foody Fuel," Koa suddenly gains an obsession with macaroni and cheese, and claims that it's the only thing he eats now. At the end of the episode, he relents and decides to eat a granola bar.

characters.name: Mary and Lizzie Goat
characters.description: Finishing Each Other's Sentences:Mary: I got my green dress dirty...Lizzie: ...so she had to wear a blue one. Furry Female Mane: They are goats with curly red hair. Girly Girl with a Tomboy Streak: Though they act very feminine, they are good at sports, and they play on the baseball team in "Ari's Muscles." Identical Twin ID Tag: In "One of These Goats," there is a substitute teacher, Mr. Hamster, teaching Elinor's class. He explains to Elinor and her friends how he can quickly remember the students that he meets by identifying what makes them different. It seems, however, that he may at first have trouble telling apart the goat twins Mary and Lizzie, who are identical. Mary usually wears a green dress and Lizzie a blue dress, but today they're both wearing blue dresses. Elinor, Olive and Ari, however, make careful observations of the two during playtime and discover several other subtle differences between the two, such as the slightly differing lengths of their horns and their differently-patterned hair accessories. Polar Opposite Twins: Mary likes to dance but Lizzie really doesn’t.

characters.name: Siggy Squirrel
characters.description: Rodent Overbite: Being a squirrel, he has buckteeth. Scout-Out: He is a part of "Squirrel Scouts," where he learned how to tie a knot. Stealth Expert: Siggy is really good at not being noticed, by being quiet, avoiding stepping on things that would make sounds, and moving slowly. Stealth Hi/Bye: Throughout "Teacher's Day," Siggy pops up from out of nowhere when the kids are talking amongst themselves, startling them. Afterwards, he disappears with no warning. Stock Animal Diet: He likes eating nuts, such as acorns, and has a picture of a peanut on his shirt.

characters.name: Ms. Mole
characters.description: Cool Teacher: She knows how to talk to kids on their level and make learning fun for them. It's for this reason that she's Elinor's favorite teacher. Disembodied Eyebrows: She has floating eyebrows when shown in profile. Eyes Always Shut: Or squinted, it's hard to tell, but Ms. Mole always has her eyes closed, even with her glasses. Furry Female Mane: She has curly red hair. Furry Reminder: Like a real mole, she has poor eyesight, which is why she wears glasses and her eyes are always closed/squinted. She uses her other senses to help her navigate.
